title: Upload a CSV and Start Your First Datilio Analysis
summary: Step-by-step: upload a CSV, preview columns, and confirm row counts before charting in Datilio.

content.summary: Step-by-step: upload a CSV, preview columns, and confirm row counts before charting in Datilio.

body.text: Your first Datilio session should take minutes, not a setup weekend. Start with a CSV you already use at work. Open Datilio and choose your CSV file. The preview screen shows column names, inferred types, and total row count—catch renamed headers or blank rows immediately.

body.alt: Analytics dashboard with charts on a laptop screen
body.caption: Analytics dashboard with charts on a laptop screen. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Open Datilio and choose your CSV file. The preview screen shows column names, inferred types, and total row count—catch renamed headers or blank rows immediately.
Scroll a sample of rows to verify dates look like dates and amounts are numeric. Fix the source file if types look wrong, then re-upload.

items: Confirm delimiter and encoding if preview looks garbled.
Note primary key columns for later filters.
Check for completely empty columns you can ignore.

body.text: Pick a numeric column and a category column, then create a bar chart to see top contributors. Adjust sort order to match how you think about the business question.
Save a filter if you only want active records or a date range—you will reuse it next week on a fresh export.
Once comfortable with upload and preview, explore Talk to Data on the same file to ask questions in plain language—after you trust what you see in the chart.

meta_description: Step-by-step: upload a CSV, preview columns, and confirm row counts before charting in Datilio.
